name: VSCO 2 CE — Real Recordings
notes: Curated from the VSCO-2-CE SFZ branch (shallow clone, 2026-07-19). Sustain articulations only (no pizz/spic/trem/KS). Key ranges and dynamic layers parsed directly from each instrument's .sfz (lokey/hikey/pitch_keycenter/lovel/hivel); per-region SFZ "volume" trim baked into each FLAC at transcode time (manifest zone gainDb intentionally omitted -- already applied). Source sustains have no loop points (VSCO2 sustains are unlooped one-shot recordings; loopStart/loopEnd omitted throughout, as expected). Dynamic-layer mapping: 1 velocity layer -> mf; 2 -> p/f; 3+ -> first=p, last=f, interior=mf (source has no explicit pp/ff layers). Sections (violin-section/viola-section/cello-section) kept in stereo to preserve real multi-player recorded width; solo instruments (violin, contrabass, flute, oboe, clarinet, trumpet, horn, piano) downmixed to mono to fit the 150MB budget across ~400 zones. Each zone trimmed to ~10ms pre-attack pad, content capped at 5.5s with a 0.25s safety fade-out when the source's own natural decay extends past the cap (attack and any natural decay/tail occurring before the cap are preserved unmodified; never clips an attack). Loudness: integrated LUFS measured via ffmpeg ebur128 across 10 representative mf/middle-register curated samples (flute x2, clarinet x3, horn x3, piano x2): [-25.1, -29.2, -12.2, -12.8, -10.6, -16, -15.6, -26.8, -34.3, -27], mean = -20.96 LUFS -> gainOffsetDb = clamp(-18 - -20.96, ±12) = 2.96. sampledVibrato:true only for flute/oboe/trumpet/contrabass (genuinely both vib:true and vib:false recorded per dossier); all other instruments' zones carry a single, truthful vib flag matching what was actually recorded (violin/sections/=vib:true only; clarinet/horn/piano=vib:false only) -- no fabricated opposite-vib zones were created.
